metadata.title:
SPRAGUE


JOB TIED


Up AGAIN

Central Labor Council to
Consider City's Official
Actions Against


Labor.

pages.raw_text:
Because of the continued opposl-
tion of the city administration to
the cause of union labor, and par
ticularly the offensive attitude of
City Commissioner George w. Arm
strong to all union men, the bridge
mems union will ask the Central
Labor council at its meeting next
Monday night and the Building
Trades council at its meeting Tues
day night to put the city of Spo-
kane, as an employer, on the unfair
list.


This action was taken at a meet
ing of the Bridge and Structural
Iron Workers local last night, at
which time all work under the city S
dispensation was placed on the un'
fair list. Two committees were ap-
pointed. one to visit and urge SINH
lar action by the trades council. and
the other to sec the building trades
council.


MAY TIE UP WATER WORKS


If such an order is adopted by
the central organizations ii will be
far reaching in effect. 1t will mean
the calling ont of all the citys
water department employees who
hold union cards. and the bulk of
them are connected with one union
or another. Vt will mean that book
keepers and clerks at the city hall
